artifacts.summary: Cantor pairing dovetailing infrastructure with ProcessObligation type and coverage theorem
Dovetailed staged build with DovetailedPoint, state machine, monotonicity, linearity proofs. Two sorries for has_future/has_past.
Progress summary documenting Phase 1-3 completion and Phase 4 blocking issue
Research report analyzing blocker resolution via density argument with F^i(neg bot) encodings

partial_progress.details: Phase 1-3 complete. Phase 4 blocker analyzed and resolution identified: density argument using F^i(neg bot) with large encodings.

research_findings.key_insight: While pair(L, k) may be < s for the specific encoding k of phi, the density axiom provides F^i(neg bot) in every MCS for all i, with encodings growing unboundedly. For large enough i, pair(L, k_i) > n, ensuring p exists when the obligation is processed.
required_lemmas: encoding_unbounded: Show F^i(neg bot) encodings grow without bound
density_iterate_in_mcs: F(phi) in M implies F^n(phi) in M by induction
witness_at_large_step: Large encoding means witness added after p enters
pair_lower_bound: pair(a,b) >= a + b (may be in Mathlib)
research_findings.mathematical_soundness: Verified - density axiom is part of dense logic, iteration follows by induction, CanonicalR preserved through executeForwardStep

next_steps: Implement the 4 required lemmas in DovetailedBuild.lean, then complete has_future and has_past proofs
